name: git_init
description: Initializes a new Git repository in the specified project directory. This is typically the first step in version controlling a project. It creates a .git subdirectory.

project_path.description: The root directory path of the project where the Git repository should be initialized.

initial_branch_name.description: Optional name for the initial branch. Defaults to 'main'. Some Git versions might use 'master' if not specified, but 'main' is a common modern default.

hints.description: This tool is idempotent (running it multiple times on an already initialized repository typically has no adverse effect or reinitializes safely) and non-destructive, but it does create a .git directory.
